data.narrative:
Raphe mPhibr is a Series B-stage company in Defense & Security that has raised $145M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with primary focus on 6 same-sector, same-stage private comparables including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $145M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $276M. Peer-median analysis extracts valuations from the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which range from $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns) to $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security), with a median of $1.2B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $234M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $738M. The wide spread reflects material tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to capital raised, and peer comparables, which imply substantially higher valuations for Series B-stage defense-tech firms. Key uncertainties include the applicability of public-market comparables to this private stage, differentiation within the peer set, and whether recent capital raises by comparable firms reflect market consensus or represent outliers. Confidence in any single point estimate is limited absent revenue or profitability data.
